---
title: "isa401_final_project"
author: "Nathan Rudy"
date: "2026-04-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Web Scraping, Wikipedia Article

```{r}
disaster_url <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_natural_disasters_in_the_United_States"

robotstxt::paths_allowed(disaster_url)
```

```{r}
# Step 1
resp <- httr2::request(
  disaster_url
  ) |>
  httr2::req_user_agent("An ISA 401 Bot from Miami University") |>
  httr2::req_perform()

disaster_webpage <- resp |> httr2::resp_body_raw() |> rvest::read_html()

disaster_webpage |> 
  rvest::html_elements("table.wikitable") |> 
  rvest::html_table() -> disaster_table

print(disaster_table)
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(rvest)
library(httr2)
library(purrr)
library(tigris)   # install.packages("tigris") if needed

disaster_url <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_natural_disasters_in_the_United_States"

resp <- httr2::request(disaster_url) |>
  httr2::req_user_agent("An ISA 401 Bot from Miami University") |>
  httr2::req_perform()

disaster_webpage <- resp |> httr2::resp_body_raw() |> rvest::read_html()

disaster_webpage |>
  rvest::html_elements("table.wikitable") |>
  rvest::html_table() -> disaster_table

# ── 1. Combine & clean ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

combined <- bind_rows(disaster_table)

combined <- combined |>
  rename(
    year     = `Year`,
    type     = `Disaster`,
    damage   = `Damage costUS$`,
    location = `Location`
  )

# ── 2. Convert damage string → numeric (USD) ─────────────────────────────────

parse_damage <- function(x) {
  x <- str_to_lower(str_trim(x))

  # Return NA for unknown/none entries
  if (x %in% c("", "unknown", "n/a", "none", "minimal", "—", "-")) return(NA_real_)

  # Strip citation brackets like [12], dollar signs, commas, spaces
  x <- str_remove_all(x, "\\[.*?\\]|\\$|,|\\s")

  # Extract the numeric part and the multiplier word
  number    <- as.numeric(str_extract(x, "[0-9]+\\.?[0-9]*"))
  multiplier <- case_when(
    str_detect(x, "trillion") ~ 1e12,
    str_detect(x, "billion")  ~ 1e9,
    str_detect(x, "million")  ~ 1e6,
    str_detect(x, "thousand") ~ 1e3,
    TRUE                      ~ 1       # already a raw number
  )

  number * multiplier
}

cleaned <- combined |>
  mutate(year = as.numeric(str_extract(year, "\\d{4}"))) |>
  filter(!is.na(year), year >= 2000) |>
  select(year, type, damage, location) |>
  mutate(damage_usd = map_dbl(damage, parse_damage)) |>
  select(-damage) |>                      # drop original string column
  mutate(location = str_split(location, ";|\\band\\b")) |>
  unnest(location) |>
  mutate(location = str_trim(location)) |>
  filter(location != "")

# ── 3. Build a state-name → FIPS lookup from tigris ─────────────────────────
#
# tigris::fips_codes is a built-in dataframe with every state & county FIPS.
# We'll use it to match state names and abbreviations found in location strings.

data("fips_codes", package = "tigris")   # columns: state, state_code, state_name,
                                          #          county_code, county, county_fips

state_lookup <- fips_codes |>
  select(state_name, state_abb = state, state_fips = state_code) |>
  distinct()

# Full US state names and abbreviations for detection
all_state_names <- state_lookup$state_name
all_state_abbs  <- state_lookup$state_abb

# ── 4. Extract FIPS from location string ─────────────────────────────────────
#
# Strategy:
#   a) Look for a known state name or abbreviation in the location string
#   b) If "City, ST" pattern — also try Census geocoder for county-level FIPS
#   c) Otherwise return state-level FIPS only (county = NA)

get_fips_from_location <- function(loc) {

  empty <- tibble(
    input_location = loc,
    state_fips     = NA_character_,
    county_fips    = NA_character_,
    full_fips      = NA_character_,
    matched_place  = NA_character_
  )

  # ── a) Detect state name or abbreviation ───────────────────────────────────
  matched_state <- state_lookup |>
    filter(
      str_detect(loc, regex(state_name, ignore_case = TRUE)) |
      str_detect(loc, regex(paste0("\\b", state_abb, "\\b"), ignore_case = TRUE))
    ) |>
    slice(1)   # take first match if multiple

  if (nrow(matched_state) == 0) return(empty)

  sfips <- matched_state$state_fips
  sname <- matched_state$state_name

  # ── b) Try Census geocoder if pattern looks like "City, State" ─────────────
  if (str_detect(loc, ",")) {
    city  <- str_trim(str_extract(loc, "^[^,]+"))
    state <- str_trim(str_extract(loc, "(?<=,).+$"))

    geo_resp <- tryCatch({
      httr2::request("https://geocoding.geo.census.gov/geocoder/geographies/address") |>
        httr2::req_url_query(
          city      = city,
          state     = state,
          benchmark = "Public_AR_Current",
          vintage   = "Current_Current",
          format    = "json",
          layers    = "Counties"
        ) |>
        httr2::req_user_agent("An ISA 401 Bot from Miami University") |>
        httr2::req_throttle(rate = 5) |>
        httr2::req_perform()
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    if (!is.null(geo_resp)) {
      result <- tryCatch(geo_resp |> httr2::resp_body_json(), error = function(e) NULL)
      geo    <- tryCatch(
        result$result$addressMatches[[1]]$geographies$Counties[[1]],
        error = function(e) NULL
      )

      if (!is.null(geo)) {
        return(tibble(
          input_location = loc,
          state_fips     = geo$STATE,
          county_fips    = geo$COUNTY,
          full_fips      = geo$GEOID,   # 5-digit: state + county
          matched_place  = geo$NAME
        ))
      }
    }
  }

  # ── c) Fallback: state-level FIPS only ─────────────────────────────────────
  tibble(
    input_location = loc,
    state_fips     = sfips,
    county_fips    = NA_character_,
    full_fips      = NA_character_,   # can't make a valid 5-digit without county
    matched_place  = sname
  )
}

# ── 5. Apply to unique locations only (saves API calls) ──────────────────────

unique_locations <- unique(cleaned$location)

fips_lookup <- map(unique_locations, get_fips_from_location) |>
  bind_rows()

# ── 6. Join back & finalize ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

final <- cleaned |>
  left_join(fips_lookup, by = c("location" = "input_location")) |>
  select(
    year,
    type,
    damage_usd,
    input_location = location,
    matched_place,
    state_fips,
    county_fips,
    full_fips
  )

print(final)
```
